title: Roulinia unifaria
taxonomy: Roulinia unifaria is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Brazil. Roulinia unifaria is classified as a legume, a type of plant that produces flowers in clusters and has compound leaves. The exact classification of Roulinia unifaria is further specified as belonging to the subfamily Faboideae. This classification places it within the larger family of legumes.
uses: Roulinia unifaria, a plant native to South America, is often used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses.
